seoTitle: How to Test Microphone on Microsoft Teams Easily (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to check your microphone in Microsoft Teams using the Device Settings panel and the Test Call bot before joining a live meeting.
intro: Nothing derails a client pitch faster than discovering your mic isn't working right when you're supposed to start speaking. This guide covers navigating to the Devices settings panel, running a diagnostic Test Call with Teams' Echo bot, and checking the pre-join screen's audio visualizer before entering a live meeting. It also covers turning on background noise suppression for clearer calls.

steps.heading: Open device settings
steps.body: Click the settings and more ellipsis icon next to your profile avatar, then select the Devices category.

steps.heading: Select your microphone
steps.body: Expand the Microphone dropdown to view and select your active audio input hardware.

steps.heading: Run a test call
steps.body: Click Make a Test Call under audio settings to launch the private recording console and follow the Test Call Bot's voice prompts.

steps.heading: Review the playback
steps.body: Listen to the immediate echo playback to verify your volume and clarity, then check the final status report for driver or latency issues.

steps.heading: Adjust sensitivity and check pre-join
steps.body: Toggle Automatically Adjust Mic Sensitivity on, and use the Test Mic and Speaker option on the meeting pre-join screen before every call.

faq.question: How do I run a Test Call in Microsoft Teams?
faq.answer: Go to Settings, then Devices, and click Make a Test Call to record a short diagnostic message and hear it played back automatically.

faq.question: What if my microphone still sounds muffled after testing?
faq.answer: Check that the correct input device is selected in the Microphone dropdown, and review your operating system's privacy permissions for microphone access.

faq.question: Can I test my mic before joining a meeting, not just from settings?
faq.answer: Yes, the pre-join landing screen includes a dedicated Test Mic and Speaker option you can use right before entering a call.

faq.question: Does Teams offer background noise suppression?
faq.answer: Yes, you can toggle on intelligent voice isolation and background noise suppression to improve clarity in noisy environments.
